Our Impact Community Giving Community Giving Strengthening Communities Worldwide FY25 Giving Fact Sheet At Enterprise Mobility, we’re focused on strengthening communities one neighborhood at a time. We do this by fostering a culture of community engagement and funding programs that drive social mobility, steward the environment and empower the communities where we live and work. By leveraging our presence in thousands of neighborhoods, we can respond to natural disasters and help address challenges, such as hunger and social inequity, through partnerships, a grants program, volunteerism and team member giving. How We Make A Difference Volunteerism Through the “My Purpose. My Time.” benefit, team members can devote a paid day to volunteer for an organization or cause of their choice. Giving Campaigns Team members can support specific organizations through annual giving campaigns as well as other charities year-round via payroll giving programs. Grants Program Team members who are actively engaged with a local nonprofit have the opportunity to apply for a charitable grant on that organization’s behalf each year. Quick Facts $113+ million donated to charitable organizations worldwide in FY25 by Enterprise Mobility through its operating groups and the Enterprise Mobility Foundation $16+ million raised in FY25 through the Stronger Together Employee Giving Campaign to support United Way and other nonprofit organizations in North America Previous 1 2 Next Partnerships & Initiatives Since the beginning, Enterprise Mobility has strived to be an engaged corporate citizen that makes a meaningful difference in the communities where we live and work. The Enterprise Mobility Foundation oversees and funds a variety of initiatives focused on creating positive impacts through long-term partnerships with nonprofit organizations. Fill Your Tank® Food insecurity exists everywhere. Through Enterprise Mobility’s Fill Your Tank program, we are working to support food banks and food programs that reach millions of people worldwide each year. Learn More ROAD Forward Enterprise Mobility's ROAD Forward initiative supports organizations addressing social equity gaps in communities across North America and Europe. Learn More Reforestation & Urban Forestry In partnership with the Arbor Day Foundation, we are helping regenerate and restore vital forest ecosystems as well as supporting tree planting projects in our local communities. Learn More Routes and Roots® Healthy Rivers Project The Routes and Roots Healthy Rivers Project supports The Nature Conservancy’s efforts to protect and restore freshwater ecosystems, preserve watershed habitat and keep pollution out of rivers worldwide. Learn More Disaster Relief When hurricanes, tornadoes, earthquakes and other devastating events strike, we strive to help fund relief efforts and support local communities in partnership with the American Red Cross. Learn More Supporting Military & Veteran Families We work with the Fisher House Foundation to help provide no-cost housing for military and veteran families while a loved one is in the hospital as well as scholarships for children of fallen or disabled service members. Learn More ROAD Forward Through our ROAD Forward initiative, we’re supporting organizations that promote social equity in the communities we serve. Learn More Fill Your Tank We recognize that food insecurity is a complex issue in many communities around the world. Our Impact Community Giving Community Giving Strengthening Communities Worldwide FY25 Giving Fact Sheet At Enterprise Mobility, we’re focused on strengthening communities one neighborhood at a time. We do this by fostering a culture of community engagement and funding programs that drive social mobility, steward the environment and empower the communities where we live and work. By leveraging our presence in thousands of neighborhoods, we can respond to natural disasters and help address challenges, such as hunger and social inequity, through partnerships, a grants program, volunteerism and team member giving. How We Make A Difference Volunteerism Through the “My Purpose. My Time.” benefit, team members can devote a paid day to volunteer for an organization or cause of their choice. Giving Campaigns Team members can support specific organizations through annual giving campaigns as well as other charities year-round via payroll giving programs. Grants Program Team members who are actively engaged with a local nonprofit have the opportunity to apply for a charitable grant on that organization’s behalf each year. The Enterprise Mobility Foundation oversees and funds a variety of initiatives focused on creating positive impacts through long-term partnerships with nonprofit organizations. Fill Your Tank® Food insecurity exists everywhere. Through Enterprise Mobility’s Fill Your Tank program, we are working to support food banks and food programs that reach millions of people worldwide each year. Learn More ROAD Forward Enterprise Mobility's ROAD Forward initiative supports organizations addressing social equity gaps in communities across North America and Europe. Learn More Reforestation & Urban Forestry In partnership with the Arbor Day Foundation, we are helping regenerate and restore vital forest ecosystems as well as supporting tree planting projects in our local communities. Learn More Routes and Roots® Healthy Rivers Project The Routes and Roots Healthy Rivers Project supports The Nature Conservancy’s efforts to protect and restore freshwater ecosystems, preserve watershed habitat and keep pollution out of rivers worldwide. Learn More Disaster Relief When hurricanes, tornadoes, earthquakes and other devastating events strike, we strive to help fund relief efforts and support local communities in partnership with the American Red Cross. Learn More Supporting Military & Veteran Families We work with the Fisher House Foundation to help provide no-cost housing for military and veteran families while a loved one is in the hospital as well as scholarships for children of fallen or disabled service members. Learn More ROAD Forward Through our ROAD Forward initiative, we’re supporting organizations that promote social equity in the communities we serve. Learn More Fill Your Tank We recognize that food insecurity is a complex issue in many communities around the world. Learn More

About Us About Us FY25 Fact Sheet For nearly 70 years, we’ve been moving mobility forward. What began with a small fleet of cars in 1957 has expanded to a network of mobility solutions that serves customers in more than 90 countries and territories around the world. Over the years, we’ve learned moving forward isn’t a simple matter of going from point A to B. Today, mobility is the movement of people and goods. It goes beyond the vehicles we drive to encompass an integrated way of travel that meets every individual’s unique needs and preferences. That is why it’s our vision to be the world’s best and most trusted mobility company. As mobility continues to evolve, we'll continue to innovate to work toward a reimagined mobility future that is more accessible, efficient and sustainable for all. Quick Facts $39 billion in 2025 fiscal-year revenue 7th-largest private U.S. company by revenue (Forbes 2024) 90,000+ global team members In 90+ countries & territories 9,500+ global rental branches 2.4 million+ vehicles in global fleet Previous 1 2 3 4 5 6 Next What Drives Us Through our global team members, discover an organization brimming with community, opportunity, innovation and, above all, mobility — in everything we do. Learn More Our Heritage How does a fleet of seven cars become a global mobility leader spanning six continents? Read the story of how our organization was built nearly 70 years ago. Learn More People & Culture From the relationship-driven work we perform every day to the communities we support through our philanthropic efforts, our people make this business thrive. | Mission | What We Can Do | Partner Charities | Your Specialist | Resources | Contact Philanthropy International Building Financial Security for Your Charity Scroll Serving Charities We assist charities so they can build financial security . We serve charities so that charities can do what they do best. Learn More Our Mission Building stronger communities, by serving charities and securing lasting family legacies. We accomplish this by: 1) Serving charities , helping them to acquire large gifts that help secure long-term funding. 2) Helping individuals and families increase their income and tax-efficiently move assets to their children.  This is accomplished by redirecting their tax dollars into charitable dollars to impact their local community and beyond. 3) Connecting trained financial advisors to charities . Our advisors are trained in innovative and cutting edge approaches to tax-exempt planning, and uniting them with charities who need their support. Learn More Our Impact Why Formal Planning? Today there are more than 1.8 million charities in the United States and 98% have no formal planned giving program. Talk to a Specialist Our Passion Our passion is to help charities concentrate on carrying out their core mission without the daily concerns of funding. We help charities learn how to solicit, attract, and tap into larger funding sources through tax-exempt planning. With our help, charities can do this without incurring huge staffing and operational costs. Learn More Elevate “We were able to elevate planned giving as an integral and essential component of our overall development program at the Temple.” -Jay Epstein at Stephen S. Wise Temple Engage "You have provided us with new ideas, new ways to identify planned giving leads, and a fresh perspective on how we can engage our planned giving prospects." -Joseph Watkins at Pt. Loma Nazarene University Empower " We have been able to teach our children how to be generous, without 'giving away' all of their inheritance.  We were able to leave a legacy in many ways, both to our children and to a charity" -Joe & Linda Next Steps The Philanthropy International (PI) Charity team would love to talk with you about how your organization can take advantage of this program. Let’s talk to discuss how you can “get your fair share” of the planned gifts that are available! Contact us By filling out the form below, you take the next step in learning how Philanthropy International has the solution: a turn-key program designed to only take one-hour a week to implement and manage.  This program also includes a custom website. Open Form got a question Today there are more than 1.8 million charities in the United States, of these, 98% have no formal planned giving program. What is "formal planned giving"? Is PI right for us? Why do we need to work with an advisor? We desire to "help you do what you do best." Contact us and ask a question. Open Form Learn More Few charities are equipped to handle the more complicated gifts often discussed in planned giving, and few have the  money and staff time necessary to start a planned giving program. Learn how we can lend a helping hand... | Mission | What We Can Do | Partner Charities | Your Specialist | Resources | Contact Philanthropy International Building Financial Security for Your Charity Scroll What We Can Do to Serve Your Charity Today there are more than 2.5 million charities in the United States. Of these charities, 98% have no formal planned giving program . Few charities are equipped to handle the  more complicated gifts often discussed in planned giving. Still fewer have the money and staff necessary to start and maintain a planned giving program. WE CAN HELP. Lending a helping hand Most of the charities we know and serve are working hard to fulfill their core mission. They often don’t have the resources and expertise to put together the essential elements of a successful planned giving program; that’s where we come in. Helping Charities Do what they do best Our passion is to help charities concentrate on carrying out their core mission without the daily concerns of funding. We help them learn how to solicit, attract, and tap into larger funding sources through tax-exempt planning. With our help, they can do this without incurring huge staffing and operational costs. Doing the planned giving heavy lifting Philanthropy International provides a lead generation strategy to identify potential planned giving donors from within the charities donor database.  We also supply the charity with professionally produced planned giving media presentations and marketing materials that deliver a compelling, consistent, and fiscally conservative message to their donors. Philanthropy International educates donors on how taking the time to plan a gift will help them build a lasting legacy of significance. They will discover that charity and tax are two sides of the same coin and that by taking the time to plan a gift, donors are able to redirect money to their favorite charity. Many times, this process will provide an increase in net worth or spendable income as well as provide a significant inheritance to those they love. To sum it up, donors will learn how to convert dollars that would have otherwise been tax, into gifts for their favorite charities. Find those planned gifts today! CONTACT US TODAY TO SPEAK WITH SOMEONE WHO IS BOTH KNOWLEDGEABLE AND PASSIONATE ABOUT HELPING CHARITIES ESCAPE THE RUT OF FUNDRAISING AND BEGIN A SUCCESSFUL PLANNED GIVING PROGRAM. YOU CAN CALL US DIRECTLY AT 909-625-4511 OR CONTACT US VIA EMAIL . Learn About Some of the Charities We have Helped What We Can Do to Empowering Individuals and Families A Family Legacy Why a Family Legacy? You’ve worked hard all of your life. You’ve reached an incredible point. You’ve provided for your family and are now ready to make a difference in the lives of others. The decision to give is the easy one. Deciding how best to proceed is a little more complicated. You want to keep things simple but remain involved. Most importantly, you want your charities to get the full benefit of your generosity. What about tax and legal issues, the abundance of paperwork, and the incredible amount of time that would be required to manage your own philanthropic program? There is an easier way. By establishing your own family legacy, commonly known as a Donor Advised Fund (DAF), you can carry out your philanthropic vision with efficiency and simplicity. For more information, submit this simple form for more information. Family Foundations Most individuals with any substantial assets and/or property struggle with tax issues. Imagine how it would make you feel if you had the choice to direct your tax dollars to your favorite charitable causes and even have a voice in how they would be spent. Does this sound too good to be true? Well, in certain situations this is  exactly the kind of choice that Congress has offered you through the special opportunities of tax-exempt planning. Philanthropy International is committed to helping individuals discover these opportunities, creating a long-term strategy to impact their community through charitable gifts, while retaining their assets for distribution to their family. Establishing Your Family Legacy with Philanthropy International Millions of Americans each year will participate in some type of planned giving program that provides increased income and/or substantial tax benefits for their family. These planned gifts are also a tremendous benefit to the charitable community. By making direct or planned gifts for a family legacy, the donor is able to enhance the benefit of these gifts. For instance, a family foundation can help foster generosity, community spirit, compassion, family unity and charitable stewardship in a family. This is why many donors choose to set up foundations early in life so that they can instill the charitable values of their family in their children. Home > Grantmaking > Grantmaking Guidelines Grantmaking Guidelines The Cynthia and George Mitchell Foundation funds highâ€impact projects in the state of Texas at the nexus of environmental protection, social equity, and economic vibrancy. To accomplish our goals, we develop long-term, strategic guidelines for our grantmaking and make disciplined and intentional funding decisions based on these guidelines. We continually monitor and adjust our strategies to accommodate the evolving accomplishments and opportunities in the areas in which we work. Current sustainability grantmaking programs focus on the following initiatives: Clean Energy> Land Conservation> Subsurface Energy> Sustainability Education> Water> To learn more about our current or previous initiatives, visit the Programs section of this website. The following guidelines will help applicants determine if a proposed project fits within the foundation’s grantmaking strategies. As a general rule, applicants should base their determination on three criteria. The proposed project must align with a specific foundation program; focus on the state of Texas; and clearly demonstrate how the project supports the relevant foundation grantmaking strategy. What We Do Not Fund The foundation's grantmaking has several limitations: In general, the foundation is unable to provide grants to organizations that are not classified as 501(c)(3) public charities by the Internal Revenue Service. The foundation does not support research unless it contains an explicit and practical policy application or outcome. Projects that are viewed favorably by the foundation will contain a viable and sophisticated communications strategy for rolling out the results of the funded effort. The foundation is unable to make grants intended to support candidates for political office or to lobby in support of or against legislation. The foundation does not fund the research, development, commercialization, or demonstration of technology. The foundation does not fund demonstration projects. GRANTMAKING LIMITATIONS The Foundation’s grantmaking has several limitations: In general, the Foundation is unable to provide grants to organizations that are not classified as 501(c)(3) public charities by the Internal Revenue Service. Home > Grantmaking > Funding Process Funding Process After reviewing our grantmaking programs and practices, and if you are confident that your organization and project is a clear and demonstrable match with the foundation’s funding priorities, you may complete and send a Letter of Inquiry (LOI), the first step in requesting funding from the foundation. For new users, please click on the Letter of Inquiry hyperlink below to create an account and submit your LOI. This should include basic information about the organization you represent and a brief description of the project for which you are seeking funding. Username and password information will be generated and forwarded to you once your account has been approved by the foundation. You do not need to discuss your LOI with staff before submission. If you have already spoken to foundation staff about your request, please state this in the LOI. We will reply to your online submission within 45 days. Due to the volume of submissions we receive, we cannot respond to phone calls or emails requesting the status of submissions. If the foundation determines that your LOI is consistent with our strategy, you will be invited to submit a full proposal. â€¨ Please note that the foundation’s grantmaking capacity is extremely limited in relation to the large number of worthwhile requests received. We are able to fund only a small fraction of requests, and a rejection is not a reflection on the value of your project or organization. The Cynthia and George Mitchell Foundation appreciates your efforts and dedication to creating a sustainable Texas. Please note that the foundation’s grantmaking capacity is extremely limited in relation to the large number of worthwhile requests received.
